steps.title: Find the slope using the two data points
steps.content:
Since demand is a linear function of price, the rate of change (slope) is constant. Using the two given points:
$$\text{slope} = \frac{15,000 - 20,000}{60 - 40} = \frac{-5,000}{20} = -250$$
The demand decreases by 250 units for every dollar increase in price.

steps.title: Determine the price change from a known point
steps.content:
We know the demand at $P = 40$ dollars. To reach the target price of $P = 55$ dollars, the price increase is:
$$55 - 40 = 15 \text{ dollars}$$

steps.title: Apply the slope to find the demand at the target price
steps.content:
At a slope of $-250$ units per dollar, a 15 dollar increase in price corresponds to a change in demand of:
$$\Delta Q = 15 \times (-250) = -3,750 \text{ units}$$
Starting from 20,000 units at $P = 40$ dollars:
$$Q = 20,000 - 3,750 = 16,250 \text{ units}$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
